mechanism_narrative: Rising real GDP, as measured by GDPC1, signals expanding aggregate demand and improving corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon as the earnings growth is capitalized into equity prices.
failure_condition: If the 60-day forward return correlation with GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth is not significantly positive over any 5-year rolling window (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6%, elevated joblessness depresses consumer spending and corporate earnings expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as risk aversion increases.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day forward return when UNRATE > 6% is not significantly negative (one-sample t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous condition of near-zero federal funds rate and extremely high unemployment indicates a liquidity trap where monetary policy is exhausted and fiscal stimulus insufficient, causing persistent weakness in corporate earnings and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the joint condition (FEDFUNDS < 0.5% and UNRATE > 8%) does not precede significantly negative 20-day returns (rank IC not negative at 90% confidence over rolling 3-year windows).

mechanism_narrative: During the post-recession early expansion phase, rising retail sales (RSAFS) signal improving consumer confidence and discretionary spending, which boosts corporate revenues and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: If in months identified as early expansion (UNRATE falling from peak, FEDFUNDS near zero), the one-month change in RSAFS does not have a statistically significant positive correlation with 20-day forward returns (Spearman rho > 0.15, p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts below 600 thousand units signals a severe contraction in residential investment and consumer wealth, which propagates to broader economic weakness and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day forward return when HOUST < 600 is not significantly negative (one-sample t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production stagnates while retail sales continue to grow, the economy is importing finished goods to satisfy demand, which weakens domestic profit margins and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If the interaction term (INDPRO * RSAFS) does not have a statistically significant negative coefficient in a linear regression predicting 60-day forward returns (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month increases in nonfarm payroll employment reflect a strengthening labor market that boosts aggregate income and consumer spending, translating into positive S&P 500 returns over short weekly horizons as sentiment improves.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return does not exhibit a positive Spearman rank correlation with the monthly change in PAYEMS (rho < 0.10 over any 3-year rolling window).

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle when the federal funds rate exceeds 1%, further rate increases compress equity valuations through higher discount rates and dampened economic activity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: If when FEDFUNDS > 1% and the Fed is in a tightening cycle (FEDFUNDS rising over past 6 months), the average 60-day forward return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).
